summarization.a: Line chart showing InGaAs etch depth as a function of acceleration grid voltage for a fixed Cl species exposure time of 15 min. Etching is negligible below 10 V and increases sharply between 15 and 30 V, indicating a threshold-like voltage dependence.
summarization.b: Multiple line chart showing InGaAs etch depth as a function of Cl species exposure time at acceleration voltages of 15 V and 10 V. Etching proceeds more rapidly at 15 V, while the 10 V condition shows a delayed onset and substantially lower etch depth over the same time range.

b.question: In subfigure (b), does etching start immediately at 0 min for either voltage condition?
Compare subfigure (b) for 15 V vs 10 V after the initial delay—how do the etch rates differ?
b.answer: After ~10–15 min, 15 V increases much faster, reaching ~60–70 Å by ~35 min, while 10 V rises slowly to only ~10–20 Å by ~35 min. This indicates a strong voltage/ion-energy dependence of the etch step.

a.question: According to subfigure (a), what is the approximate acceleration grid voltage where measurable etching begins?
For subfigure (a), what fixed Cl species exposure time was used while sweeping the grid voltage?
